element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic offensive capability, all players begin with this
Passive defense, regenerates slowly when not firing
Core progression loop - earned per target destroyed, proportional to difficulty
Players must learn situational weapon switching; purchased between waves
Up to 5 layers purchasable; does not recharge without pickups
Novel weapons compared to genre standards; always-active firing
High-cost weapons that penetrate multiple targets; rare acquisition
One-time use, clears screen except toughest bosses; emergency tool
Appear end-of-wave or mid-wave in later difficulties
Inserted into middle of later, more difficult waves
Ground bases with multiple destructible weapon components

typical_relief_triggers: Wave completion, returning to shop menu
Collecting credit bonuses from tough enemies
typical_tension_triggers: Incoming enemy waves with predetermined paths
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual escalation per sector with wave-based structure; early waves (Bravo) feature generic military targets at moderate density; mid-game (Tango) introduces thematic variety and multi-part bosses; late-game (Outer Regions) adds mid-wave bosses and planetary variety. Difficulty scaling available via replay with increased enemy density.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Money earned from destroyed enemies; players can optimize credit accumulation by grinding specific waves with valuable weapon drops or re-selling collected weapons

difficulty_curve.early_game: Bravo Sector: Generic military installations (oil silos, bases, refinery). Barren terrain, occasional forests/rivers. Enemies on predetermined paths. Introduction to basic combat and credit accumulation. Manageable with default weapons.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tango Sector: Thematic variety (water plant, farmland, jungle, city, airbase). Night-time city wave with distinct lighting. Ancient temple destroyed mid-sector. Multi-part boss (large aircraft splitting into three modules) at sector end.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Outer Regions: Planetary environments (moon, Mars-like, ice world, volcanic world, space station). Night-side lunar wave. Component-based ground bosses with multiple destructible weapons. Boss encounters mid-wave become standard in difficulty tiers above Rookie.
notable_spikes: End of Tango Sector: Multi-part aircraft boss requiring dynamic targeting
Outer Regions mid-wave bosses: Inserted difficulty spike in advanced difficulty tiers
Replay difficulty escalation: Defeating a sector on Rookie triggers Veteran difficulty on replay, increasing enemy density and complexity

notable_level_patterns.description: Game divides 27 levels into 3 sectors of 9 waves each. Bravo Sector uses generic military targets; Tango Sector uses distinct environmental themes (water plant, jungle, city, etc.); Outer Regions uses planetary variety. Each sector has a special night wave with unique music.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tango Sector Wave 8 (illuminated city night level) vs. Outer Regions Wave 3 (dark side of moon)

notable_level_patterns.description: Early waves end with single boss encounters. Later waves (especially in higher difficulties) insert bosses mid-wave. Outer Regions emphasizes component-based ground bosses where players must destroy multiple weapon systems sequentially.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tango Sector final wave (multi-part aircraft) and Outer Regions (space station with modular defenses)

notable_level_patterns.description: Players earn credits proportional to enemy/target difficulty. Optional level quitting preserves weapon drops and damage state but forfeits in-wave credits, enabling farm loops. Sector replay available post-completion at increased difficulty, creating grinding opportunities for weapon/shield upgrades before advancing.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any early Bravo Sector wave can be farmed for weapon drops by repeatedly collecting expensive weapons and quitting

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels mix airborne flying enemies and ground targets (buildings, vehicles, turrets). Some weapons target only air or only ground, requiring players to maintain balanced loadouts or dynamically switch selectable weapons based on encounter type.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Bravo Sector (military bases with ground turrets and aerial defense) and Tango Sector (jungle with mixed aerial/ground threats)
